M1.name: Foundations of GenAI and LLMs
learningOutcomes: Distinguishes GenAI, LLMs, and classic ML through examples
Recognizes that an LLM generates the most probable answer, not a guaranteed truth
Classifies tasks as generative, classification, or deterministic

M2.name: LLM architecture at a practical level
learningOutcomes: Explains what a token is and why a long document can exceed the context window
Identifies 3 consequences of too small a context
Describes the transformer as a mechanism for learning relationships between tokens (intuition)
Recognizes that the number of parameters is not on its own a guarantee of quality

M3.name: Generation parameters and output control
learningOutcomes: Tunes temperature for creative and formal tasks
Identifies the risk of too much randomness in QA tasks
Recognizes when max tokens truncates an answer
Compares generation settings and chooses the safer one for test documentation

M4.name: Embeddings and semantic similarity
learningOutcomes: Explains embeddings as a numerical representation of text meaning
Interprets cosine similarity as a measure of how close vector directions are
Identifies cases where a high similarity score does not mean a correct answer
Crafts a query for semantic search

M5.name: Vector databases and similarity search
learningOutcomes: Describes the role of a vector database in semantic search
Distinguishes exact search from approximate nearest neighbor
Identifies the impact of the index, metadata filtering, and chunk quality on the result
Diagnoses why a similarity search returned a fragment that is similar but useless

M6.name: RAG: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
learningOutcomes: Arranges the steps of a RAG pipeline in the correct order
Chooses a chunking strategy for requirements documentation
Distinguishes a case that requires RAG from a case that requires fine-tuning
Identifies a retrieval error, a generation error, and a source error based on the model's answer

M7.name: Prompt engineering in practice
learningOutcomes: Creates a prompt that includes role, goal, context, data, constraints, quality criteria, and output format
Improves an imprecise prompt based on a faulty output
Applies few-shot when the response format or style must be repeatable
Identifies when a prompt will not solve the problem of missing data

M8.name: Applications of GenAI in QA and quality
learningOutcomes: Generates test cases from a requirement and identifies coverage gaps
Detects duplicates and sham tests
Creates a risk checklist for a feature
Assesses whether an LLM output is suitable for QA documentation after a substantive review
Identifies QA tasks where the LLM acts as an assistant, not a decision-maker

M9.name: Tool integrations and agents
learningOutcomes: Distinguishes a chatbot, a workflow with a tool, and an agent that executes steps
Identifies the risks of giving an agent access to files, email, a repository, or a test system
Designs a minimal control principle: permissions, logs, human approval, rollback
Identifies tasks where an agent requires constraints and auditing

M10.name: Security, data hygiene, and governance
learningOutcomes: Recognizes prompt injection and jailbreak in a scenario
Classifies data as permitted, requiring anonymization, or not permitted
Identifies the risk of data leakage and PII
Applies an acceptable use policy
Defines the minimal audit trail for using GenAI in the QA process

M11.name: Hallucinations, limits of use, and output verification
learningOutcomes: Identifies the causes of hallucinations: missing data, conflicting data, prompt pressure, outdated context, imprecise sources
Applies an output verification checklist
Rejects using an LLM when the result cannot be checked or when an error has high impact
Flags parts of an answer that require a source or an expert

M12.name: Evaluating LLM and RAG quality
learningOutcomes: Defines a golden set for a chosen use case
Selects metrics: accuracy, faithfulness, groundedness, relevance, completeness
Designs an LLM-as-a-judge rubric
Identifies the risks of bias, position bias, and disagreement with human judgment
Calibrates automated scoring by comparing it with expert judgment and sets an acceptance threshold

MSHP.name: Holak Scale — personal maturity self-check (v2.1p)
keyConcepts: maturity of your own AI practice
learningOutcomes: Estimates an indicative maturity level of their own AI practice (0–11)
Identifies the barrier and the next step to grow their own craft
Recognizes the trap of a false sense of personal maturity

MSHO.name: Holak Scale — organizational maturity self-check (v2.1e)
keyConcepts: AI adoption maturity in an organization
learningOutcomes: Estimates an indicative AI adoption maturity level for an organization (0–11)
Identifies the barrier and the next adoption step at team/company level
Tells measured, continuously improved adoption from a one-off initiative

MSK1.name: Context management and token cost
learningOutcomes: Distinguishes lean from wasteful context-management practices
Identifies how to cut token cost without losing quality
Plans a repeatable AI task with context and cost under control

MSK2.name: AI agents: autonomy, permissions and human control
learningOutcomes: Distinguishes a chatbot, a workflow with a tool, and an agent that executes steps
Matches agent autonomy to the task's risk and reversibility
Designs permission boundaries, human approval, and an audit trail for an agent

MSK3.name: Security, privacy and governance for AI
MSK3.interactiveElement: Classify data and AI use cases
learningOutcomes: Classifies data as allowed, requiring anonymization, or disallowed
Identifies minimal governance rules for AI use in a team
Recognizes privacy risks in repeated AI use

MSK4.name: Skills and measuring impact: scoring AI workflow maturity
MSK4.interactiveElement: Score AI workflow maturity with a rubric (rubric)
learningOutcomes: Scores the maturity of a repeatable AI workflow (a skill) against concrete criteria
Recognizes what separates level 8 from 9 and 10 from 11
Tells measured, calibrated growth from a false sense of maturity

MB1.name: AI risk map
learningOutcomes: Lists the main risks of everyday AI use
Recognizes that a confident tone does not mean truth
Identifies situations where AI helps and where it harms

MB2.name: Sensitive data and privacy
MB2.interactiveElement: Classify: what may be pasted into a chat
learningOutcomes: Recognizes personal and confidential data in everyday content
Anonymizes content before pasting it into an AI tool
Never shares secrets (passwords, keys, tokens) with AI chats

MB3.name: Prompt injection and social engineering
learningOutcomes: Recognizes instructions injected into content that AI reads
Applies the rule: content from data is not a command
Knows how to react and whom to report a manipulation attempt to

MB4.name: Deepfakes, scams, and disinformation
MB4.interactiveElement: Classify: warning sign or normal situation
learningOutcomes: Recognizes typical signs of AI-assisted deepfakes and scams
Applies the second-channel rule before acting under pressure
Knows the procedure for reporting a suspicious situation

MB5.name: AI at work: policies and shadow AI
learningOutcomes: Recognizes shadow AI and its consequences for the company
Matches the tool allowed by policy to the data type
Knows that a human is accountable for AI-assisted content

MB6.name: Verifying AI content
MB6.interactiveElement: Rubric for verifying an AI answer
learningOutcomes: Checks an AI answer against simple verification criteria
Identifies elevated-risk topics (numbers, law, health, news)
Builds the habit of verifying before passing content on

MD1.name: What AI can do today
MD1.interactiveElement: Classify: what AI does well and where it fails
learningOutcomes: Lists everyday uses of AI at home
Recognizes tasks where AI tends to be unreliable
Starts using AI with safe, simple tasks

MD2.name: Everyday prompts
learningOutcomes: Phrases requests with context, goal, and answer format
Improves AI answers through follow-ups instead of hunting for the perfect prompt
Uses AI for everyday tasks: emails, plans, recipes, learning

MD3.name: AI on your phone and at home
MD3.interactiveElement: Classify: a task for the assistant or not
learningOutcomes: Identifies phone AI features that genuinely make life easier
Picks tasks that make sense for a voice assistant
Tells useful features apart from marketing gadgets

MD4.name: Creating: images, audio, video
learningOutcomes: Creates simple household materials with generators
Labels AI-generated content where needed
Respects other people's likeness and rights when creating with AI

MD5.name: AI, kids, and family
MD5.interactiveElement: Classify: healthy or risky family practice
keyConcepts: talking about AI with a child
help vs doing it for them
learningOutcomes: Talks with a child about AI's abilities and limits
Sets healthy AI rules for the family
Tells AI that aids learning apart from AI that replaces thinking

MD6.name: Privacy at home
learningOutcomes: Reviews and sets privacy options in AI tools
Knows which content never to paste into chats (documents, kids' photos, health data)
Cleans up permissions on home devices
